nameMeanings.name: Gater
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Gaiter
meanings: English: occupational name from northern Middle English gater ‘goatherd’, a derivative of Middle English gayte ‘goat’ (from Old English gāt, with preservation of the unrounded northern ā, or from Old Norse geit). The surname is found in northern England and the Midlands, and has probably also absorbed Middle English gaythird ‘goatherd’.

English: topographic name from Middle English gater ‘dweller by the town gate or gateway’. This type of name derived from a topographic term + -er, is found mainly in southern England, especially Kent, Sussex, and Hampshire.

variants.name: Gaitor
meanings: African American: altered form of English   Gater   or   Gaiter  . This surname is also common in the Bahamas.

variants.name: Geeter
meanings: African American: possibly an altered form of English   Gater   or   Gaiter  .
Flemish (De Geeter): occupational name for a goatherd, a variant of De Geyter .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Güter (see Guter ).

variants.name: Gather
meanings: North German: habitational name for someone from either of two places called Gath (near Krefeld and in Schleswig).

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

en.lifeSummary: Mary Gater was born in 1598, in Ellesborough, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 daughter with John Debell. She died in 1650, in Milford, New Haven, Connecticut, United States, at the age of 52.

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Gater(은)는 1598년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 엘레스버루그h에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John Debell(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1650년 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 미퍼드에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.
